member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.OverlayPreviewRules
member.summary: Shared, UI-agnostic preview-rect rules used by overlay implementations to describe the rectangle highlighted while a floating window is dragged over a drop target.
member.remarks:
All methods operate on logical pixel dimensions and return offsets relative to the top-left of the area being described (a pane or the docking manager). The WPF canonical rules these mirror are:
Pane Left/Right → half the pane width.
Pane Top/Bottom → half the pane height.
Pane Inside → the full pane.
Manager Left/Right → min(preferredDockWidth, areaWidth / 2).
Manager Top/Bottom → min(preferredDockHeight, areaHeight / 2).

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.OverlayPreviewRules.TryComputePanePreviewRect(Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.DropTargetType,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Computes the preview rect for a directional pane split or an inside (tab-join) drop. All output coordinates are relative to the pane's top-left corner.
member.param: When this method returns true, the left offset of the preview rect.
When this method returns true, the top offset of the preview rect.
When this method returns true, the width of the preview rect.
When this method returns true, the height of the preview rect.
member.returns: false when is not a recognised pane drop target; otherwise true.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.OverlayPreviewRules.TryComputeManagerPreviewRect(Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.DropTargetType,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Computes the preview rect for a docking manager outer-edge drop. All output coordinates are relative to the docking manager's top-left corner.
member.param: The width of the docking manager area.
The height of the docking manager area.
The floating window's preferred dock width or height (from its dock size or actual size).
When this method returns true, the left offset of the preview rect.
When this method returns true, the top offset of the preview rect.
When this method returns true, the width of the preview rect.
When this method returns true, the height of the preview rect.
member.returns: false when is not a recognised manager drop target; otherwise true.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.OverlayTabTargetRules
member.summary: Shared, UI-agnostic tab target geometry rules used by overlay implementations to compute where a dropped item would be appended relative to the visible tabs of a pane.
member.remarks: All methods operate purely on logical pixel coordinates so the same rules can be reused across WPF and other UI front ends without depending on any presentation framework types.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.OverlayTabTargetRules.ShouldUseAsTrailingTabCandidate(System.Nullable{System.Double},System.Double)
member.summary: Determines whether a candidate tab should become the trailing (right-most) tab used to anchor an append drop area.
member.param: The right edge of the current trailing candidate, or null when none has been chosen yet.
The right edge of the candidate tab being evaluated.
member.returns: true when the candidate extends further right than the current trailing candidate (or none exists yet); otherwise false.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.OverlayTabTargetRules.TryComputeTrailingTabDropArea(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Computes the trailing (append) tab drop area immediately after the last visible tab.
member.param: The left edge of the last visible tab.
The top edge of the last visible tab.
The right edge of the last visible tab.
The bottom edge of the last visible tab.
The right boundary of the pane that hosts the tabs.
When this method returns true, the left edge of the computed drop area.
When this method returns true, the top edge of the computed drop area.
When this method returns true, the right edge of the computed drop area.
When this method returns true, the bottom edge of the computed drop area.
member.returns: false when the last tab has no width or the computed area would exceed the pane's right boundary; otherwise true.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.Shell.Standard.DoubleUtilities
member.summary: DoubleUtil uses fixed eps to provide fuzzy comparison functionality for doubles. Note that FP noise is a big problem and using any of these compare methods is not a complete solution, but rather the way to reduce the probability of repeating unnecessary work.

member.name: F:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.Shell.Standard.DoubleUtilities.Epsilon
member.summary: Epsilon - more or less random, more or less small number.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Controls.Shell.Standard.DoubleUtilities.AreClose(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: AreClose returns whether or not two doubles are "close". That is, whether or not they are within epsilon of each other. There are plenty of ways for this to return false even for numbers which are theoretically identical, so no code calling this should fail to work if this returns false.
member.returns: The result of the AreClose comparision.

member.name: P:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.DockingManager.AutoHideDelay
member.summary:
Gets or sets the wait time in milliseconds that is applicable when the system AutoHides a (reduces it to a side anchor) after the user:
1) clicks on a that is anchored in one of the property sides (top, right, left, or bottom) and
2) clicks somewhere else into a focusable element (different document).
Expected behavior: The system waits for the configured time and reduces the (into its side anchor). Recommended configuration value range should be between 0 and 1500 milliseconds.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.DockingManager.CreateUIElementForModel(Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Interfaces.ILayoutElement)
member.summary:
Method is invoked to create the actual visible UI element from a given layout model. It is invoked when:
1. New UI items are created and layed out or 2. Layout is deserialized, and the previous UI items are restored to the screen (using the model who's information was serialized to XML).
member.returns: The UI element created for the specified model, or when no element can be created.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Interfaces.IDockingManager
member.summary: UI-agnostic interface for the docking manager. Provides layout management, content sources, active content tracking, and lifecycle events without requiring WPF references.

remarks.para: Implemented by the WPF DockingManager class.
ViewModels can depend on this interface to interact with the docking system without coupling to WPF controls.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Interfaces.ILayoutEngine
member.summary:
Defines the strategy for manipulating the layout tree when docking, undocking, and rearranging anchorable panes within a .
The uses an to perform layout-tree operations without depending on the WPF visual tree.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Interfaces.ILayoutEngine.InsertPane(Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Layout.LayoutRoot,Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Layout.LayoutAnchorablePane,Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Layout.AnchorSide)
member.summary: Inserts an anchorable pane into the layout tree at the specified side. The implementation decides where exactly the pane is placed within the panel hierarchy.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Interfaces.ILayoutEngine.FindOrCreateContentPanel(Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Layout.LayoutRoot,System.Windows.Controls.Orientation)
member.summary: Finds or creates a content panel with the specified orientation. For Horizontal, this is the panel that holds left panes + document + right panes. For Vertical, this is the panel that holds top content + bottom panes.
member.param: The desired orientation of the content panel.
member.returns: The existing or newly created panel.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Interfaces.ILayoutEngine.FixSplitOrientation(Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Layout.LayoutAnchorable,Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Layout.AnchorSide)
member.summary: After docking, ensures that contiguous anchorable panes on the same side are grouped with the correct split orientation. Left/Right panes → (top-to-bottom). Top/Bottom panes → (side-by-side).
member.param: The anchorable that was just docked.
The anchor side it was docked to.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Interfaces.ILayoutEngine.EnsureBottomFullWidth(Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Layout.LayoutRoot)
member.summary: Ensures bottom panes span the full width of the layout by lifting them to the outermost vertical panel level.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Interfaces.ILayoutEngine.EnsureSidesFullHeight(Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Layout.LayoutRoot)
member.summary: Ensures side panes (left/right) span the full height of the layout by lifting them to the outermost horizontal panel level.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Interfaces.ILayoutEngine.GetDesiredOrientation(Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Layout.AnchorSide)
member.summary: Gets the desired split orientation for panes on the given anchor side.
member.returns: for Left/Right sides, for Top/Bottom sides.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Serialization.Dto.LayoutElementDto
member.summary: Abstract base DTO for all layout elements.
member.remarks: The DTOs in this file are plain data carriers for the JSON layout format and deliberately have no initializers: the serializer omits default values (false, 0, null) when writing, so a non-default initializer would corrupt the round trip when the property is omitted. The sets every property explicitly in both directions.

member.name: T:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Themes.VsThemeParser
member.summary: Parses Visual Studio .vstheme XML files and extracts color definitions from the Environment category.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Themes.VsThemeParser.Parse(System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Parses a .vstheme file from a stream and returns the color palette. The stream may contain either plain .vstheme XML or GZIP-compressed .vstheme XML; the compression is detected automatically.
member.param: The stream containing the (optionally GZIP-compressed) .vstheme XML content.
member.returns: A dictionary mapping color token names to their resolved colors.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Themes.VsThemeParser.ParseGZip(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Parses a GZIP-compressed .vstheme file from its raw compressed bytes and returns the color palette.
member.returns: A dictionary mapping color token names to their resolved colors.

member.name: M:Mosaic.UI.Wpf.AvalonDock.Themes.VsThemeParser.ParseFile(System.String)
member.summary: Parses a .vstheme file from a file path and returns the color palette.
member.returns: A dictionary mapping color token names to their resolved colors.
